rationale: Ground truth shows no substantive reviewer concerns (just LGTM approvals), so both candidates trivially satisfy recall. Y did more rigorous, precisely-cited code verification (exact line ranges like serving_chat.py:452-456, :2140, :2586) and reached an APPROVE verdict that matches the actual clean-merge outcome; its one extra comment is a hedged, low-value 'sanity check' suggestion. X's verification is similarly grounded but slightly vaguer (e.g. 'line ~430'), and its single added finding (stale README.md:58) is concrete and actionable with a specific fix, though it's a scope-creep nitpick on a file the PR didn't touch and reviewers never raised. X wins on actionability, Y wins on grounding rigor and correctness of overall verdict, making this a close call slightly favoring Y.
